Pariprashnena — Q&A Archive

A read-only archive of 1,235 questions and 14,977 answers from a Gauḍīya Vaiṣṇava forum (2007–2012).

Why do we spend so much time on the internet?

Health · asked by user [] · 2007-08-10 · 11 answers
So much of my service is internet based these days. Often i wish this were not the case. Im curious how much time other devotees spend staring at their monitors on average every day? and specifically why?

:)

.
user [78] · 2007-08-10
I begin in the morning but I do do my daily chores. Its maybe that its the time for the Internet :P
I also go on the internet for Daily Darshan of Sri Sri Radha Gopinatha (of Radhadesh).
Also to check my mails. And sometimes I chat with friends. The Internet makes it possible for devotees of other countries to associate with each other. Daily I check this site maybe a dozen times if there is a new question asked., so I can learn something. I also have to stay in touch with our Pandavasena Forum site.
Thats my daily planning on the internet. But I do get bored of it sometimes...
:)
user [2] · 2007-08-11
I live now in a remote area, w/o devotees, so it is my e-sadhu sanga, I guess.
user [19] · 2007-08-11
10 hours average.
user [106] · 2007-08-11
I have a started a personal program of rehabilitation to get away from the computer. My service is also internet based, but in all honesty this entities (+mind and senses) can keep us hooked for hours... Now I spent more time with my kids..., chanting, reading or resting....

I reckon computers tend to take away the normal and natural human interaction... My kids are growing, and one day I wont be able to take time back and might regret not having been there enough for them in every way....

Too long will be my general answer to the original question
user [95] · 2007-08-11
7 hrs a day.. its a part of my job.
Yes, sometimes do get bored of it too.
user [71] · 2007-08-11
Im planning to move soon. Im thinking of not installing a computer at all and just live with the books and japa (maybe just a phone needed for my job, otherwise it would fly also). I should be able to live without. No idea whether I will make it but it would be good.

If we can live without sex, which is so hard to overcome, why not overcome the Internet (if we dont really need it in Krsnas service)?
user [78] · 2007-08-12
>why not overcome the Internet (if we dont really need it in Krsnas service)? <

Actually we sometimes need it for Sadhu sanga like this site which is created to do that. Some devotees on this site are from India and others from America and from Spain and I am from Holland. And still we can have Sadhu sanga. I am learning a lot from this site and am very happy to get this oppurtunity and mercy of the Lord to do this. And I like to communicate with other devotees, what could be better. The only thing what devotees talk about here is: how is your school? or how is life treating you? You cant have any Sadhu sanga with them because they dont let you.
user [71] · 2007-08-12
>>The only thing what devotees talk about here is: how is your school? or how is life treating you? You cant have any Sadhu sanga with them because they dont let you.<<

You mean at Radhadesh? How is that? There are a few dozens of devotees living there...! And soon the college will start again, thats another 40 devotees coming.

On the other hand, I think I know what you mean. I have had the same experience of being among many devotees and still feeling lonely.

Always in a hurry, never any time to sit down and talk...also often because of the internet :P
user [78] · 2007-08-12
<You mean at Radhadesh?>

No, not at Radhadesh, but in Holland. Actually I am also joining this years Bhaktivedanta College.
In Radhadesh there is a very peacefull mood. Very good to chant. I do advise all you prabhujis and matajis to go there sometimes. Very nice, and very kind and wise Vaishnavas living there.
user [71] · 2007-08-13
Wow, wish you all the best and a lot of courage to complete the courses. You are going to have a great time!
user [78] · 2007-08-13
Thank you for the wishes...

Prabhuji, do you maybe know someone who has already gone to the College? I really like to know some experiences. The best would be if he/she was from last year. I hear they do some changes with each new schoolyear. So how more recent how better. Thanx anyways,
ys,
Servant Krsna
:)
